title: Two Geographies of the Afterlife (Vertical Ascent and Horizontal Journey)
description: The two spatial models that describe the same after-death process: Model 1 (Vertical Ascent, used by Tibetan, Steiner, Tantraloka, Layayoga) describes the soul rising through levels of increasing subtlety to encounter totality at the summit. Model 2 (Horizontal Journey, used by Egyptian tradition) describes the soul traveling with the solar barque through 12 hours of night. The midnight union of Re and Osiris (Hour 6) corresponds to the summit of the vertical model.
